name: loop_slice_slice_selected
description: Loop Slice 'Slice Selected' option (task 0248): when ON, the cut is RESTRICTED to the selected face region instead of the whole ring. On the default cube (makeCube ±0.5), select two adjacent faces — front (z=-0.5) and bottom (y=-0.5). Their shared edge seeds the belt ring around the X axis {front, top, back, bottom}. With Slice Selected OFF the whole belt is cut (4 new midpoints → 12 verts, 10 faces). With it ON only the two selected faces are sliced; the cut terminates watertight at the selection border — the two unselected belt neighbours (top, back) each absorb ONE terminating midpoint as an n-gon, and NO cut lands on the top-back edge (v6-v7), so only 3 new midpoints appear (11 verts, 8 faces). Reference-independent geometry law: every new vertex sits at the 0.5 midpoint of a pre-op cube edge, and the whole-ring-only midpoint [0,0.5,0.5] is provably ABSENT under Slice Selected (guaranteed by the bidirectional vertex-set match).
source: LIVE-VERIFIED (task 0263). The reference `select` (Slice Selected) behaviour was driven HEADLESSLY on the reference app (build the +-0.5 cube, select the two adjacent faces, activate the tool, set the option, and COMMIT via a real interactive viewport gesture) — the earlier 'needs a human VNC drive' claim is DISPROVEN (scripted-vs-interactive commit were cross-checked bit-identical). The reference geometry MATCHES this fixture byte-for-byte: select ON -> 11 verts / 17 edges / 8 faces with new midpoints (0,-0.5,-0.5),(0,0.5,-0.5),(0,-0.5,0.5) and the two unselected belt neighbours each absorbing a terminating midpoint as an n-gon (watertight, 'respecting corners'); select OFF (whole ring) -> 12 verts / 20 edges / 10 faces with 4 midpoints. Capture provenance in the private toolcards/mesh.loopSlice.

provenance.notes: Driven headlessly + COMMIT via a real interactive viewport gesture (scripted-vs-interactive commit cross-checked bit-identical).
